name: Yeah Yeah Yeahs
description:
'''Yeah Yeah Yeahs''' (often abbreviated as '''YYYs''') are an American [[indie rock]] band formed in [[New York City]] in 2000 by [[Karen O]] (vocals), [[Nick Zinner]] (guitars, keyboards), and [[Brian Chase]] (drums, percussion). They were key artists in the [[Rock music#Garage rock and post-punk revivals|rock movements]] and the [[Music of New York City|New York music scene]] of the 2000s, noted for their evolving musical style and energetic performances.

Yeah Yeah Yeahs began recording in 2001, releasing two [[EP]]s and one single. They found commercial success with their acclaimed debut album, ''[[Fever to Tell]]'' (2003), which put them at the forefront of the 2000s rock revival. It produced the single "[[Maps (Yeah Yeah Yeahs song)|Maps]]", which became the band's [[List of signature songs|signature song]]. Their next two albums, ''[[Show Your Bones]]'' (2006) and ''[[It's Blitz!]]'' (2009), produced the singles "[[Gold Lion]]", "[[Zero (Yeah Yeah Yeahs song)|Zero]]", and "[[Heads Will Roll (song)|Heads Will Roll]]". Their fourth album, ''[[Mosquito (album)|Mosquito]]'' (2013), became their highest-charting record on the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], at number five. After a hiatus from 2014 to 2017, they released their fifth album, ''[[Cool It Down]]'' (2022), which marked a critical resurgence. It produced the single "[[Spitting Off the Edge of the World]]".

Yeah Yeah Yeahs have received [[List of awards and nominations received by Yeah Yeah Yeahs|several accolades]], including five [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]] nominations. ''[[Rolling Stone]]'' included ''Fever to Tell'' on their list of the "[[Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time|500 Greatest Albums of All Time]]" and "Maps" on their "[[Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Songs of All Time|500 Greatest Songs of All Time]]"; the same publication also ranked Karen O and Zinner among the greatest singers and guitarists in history, respectively.
